title: symphyotrichum puniceum var. scabricaule
intro: Symphyotrichum puniceum var. scabricaule, also known as Purple Coneflower, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and distinctive appearance. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Symphyotrichum puniceum var. scabricaule include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
